caption:
8th Pay Commission arrears are on their way — but most employees don't know about the tax trap hiding inside.

When you receive salary arrears in a lump sum, the Income Tax Act treats ALL of it as income in the year you receive it. Even if those arrears cover 3 years of past service, every rupee lands in THIS year's return — potentially jumping you from the 5% slab straight to 20% or 30% overnight.

Section 89(1) is the fix. It recalculates your tax as if the arrears were spread across the years they actually belong to — so you pay only the genuine extra, not an inflated bracket bill. Illustration: on ₹5 lakh in arrears, many employees can save ₹30,000–₹60,000 using this relief (exact saving depends on your income level and the years involved — not a guarantee).

Critical: You MUST file Form 10E on the income tax portal BEFORE you submit your ITR. ITAT benches have consistently held that if Form 10E is not filed first, the relief is permanently disallowed — no retrospective fix exists.

What to do → Use the arrears-relief calculator on incometax.gov.in → File Form 10E → THEN file your ITR.

Need help getting the numbers right? WhatsApp us — link in bio.

Note: general information, not professional advice — talk to us for your specific case.

Source: Section 89(1), Income Tax Act 1961; Rule 21AA; CBDT Form 10E guidance on incometax.gov.in
